sections.context: ADR-0013 makes Creator Onboarding Interview → ProfileGen the reference vertical slice: the one flow built end-to-end so every pattern (auth, db, the @resonance/ai agent+tool registry, streaming UI, Server Actions + Zod, pgvector matching, the full test pyramid) is proven once and future flows are built by replication. It is also the first real backlog for the firstmate-only agentic workflow — decomposed along package boundaries so each step is a self-contained crewmate task in its own worktree.
sections.approach: Build the slice package-by-package, because in this monorepo package boundaries are the parallelization boundaries (ADR-0003) — one step maps to one firstmate crewmate in one treehouse worktree. Foundations first: db schema/migration and core Zod schemas + ports, which everything else consumes. Then ai (Weave interview + ProfileGen agents via the registry, ADR-0009) and ui (streaming Weave rail + Figma profile panels) proceed in parallel against those shared types. Then apps/web wires them with Zod-validated Server Actions behind an authenticated creator session (auth magic-link already landed). Finally an E2E pass proves the flow and seeds the pgvector matching backbone by embedding the generated profile (ADR-0010). Each step ships with its own tests per ADR-0011.

steps.title: db: schema + migration for users, creator_profiles, and profile embeddings
core: Zod schemas, domain types, and ports for interview messages + creator profile
ai: Weave interview agent + ProfileGen agent with saveProfile tool, plus profile embedding
ui: streaming Weave interview rail + generated-profile panels from Figma tokens
web: Zod-validated Server Actions + interview route wiring ui ↔ ai ↔ db behind creator auth
e2e + matching: Playwright flow test end-to-end and pgvector embedding/matching verification

risks: Streaming agent output wired through RSC / Server Actions is fiddly — mismatched streaming contracts between ai and ui can surface only at the web wiring step.
pgvector migration and embedding dimensionality must match the model in @resonance/ai; a dim mismatch fails silently at query time (ADR-0010).
The saveProfile agent tool crosses the ai→db boundary; transaction/ownership boundaries must be explicit so a partial generation cannot persist a half-written profile.
Figma-derived UI can drift from design tokens if built without the Figma MCP context — authorize Figma before the ui step starts.
E2E of a streaming interview is prone to flakiness; the test must assert on settled state, not intermediate tokens.
acceptance: A creator can complete the Weave interview and receive a generated profile persisted to creator_profiles.
The generated profile is embedded into pgvector, proving the matching backbone end-to-end (ADR-0010).
The interview route is gated behind an authenticated creator session.
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test pass across affected packages, and each package step ships with its own tests (ADR-0011).
A Playwright E2E drives the full flow from front door to generated profile.
